card_summary: A newspaper clipping from The Press Democrat (Santa Rosa, California), dated March 31, 1991, page 40. Santa Rosa was a city closely associated with Philip K. Dick, who lived in the area. This clipping likely contains local coverage related to Dick's legacy or community connections in Sonoma County.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from The Press Democrat (Santa Rosa, California), dated Sunday, March 31, 1991, page 40. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 9, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

The Press Democrat is the major daily newspaper for Sonoma County, California, adjacent to Marin County where Dick lived during the early 1960s. Santa Rosa is part of the broader North Bay region that formed Dick's California milieu. By 1991, Dick had been dead for nine years but his cultural presence was growing. Coverage in a North Bay newspaper may reflect local interest in a writer who had lived in the neighboring county. The specific content remains to be determined through examination of the scanned image.
